title: Session tree model — branching, forking, reintegration
purpose: Feature 22 calls for Pi-style session trees where sessions support branching, forking, reintegration, and audit trails. The current session model is flat: each agent gets its own EventStore (a Vec<SessionEvent>). Fork creates a child EventStore by copying filtered events, but there is no tree structure tracking parent-child relationships between sessions. This brief adds SessionTree to manage multiple sessions with tree relationships.
task: Create SessionTree struct managing a HashMap<SessionId, SessionNode> where each node holds an EventStore, parent link, children list, and metadata. Implement branch (create child from parent at a specific event), merge_summary (bring a child's summary back into the parent as a Compaction event), list_children, get_ancestry. Wire fork() from agent/fork.rs to register in the SessionTree instead of creating disconnected EventStores. Testing: unit tests for tree operations.

requirements.title: SessionTree struct with parent-child tracking
requirements.spec: SessionTree SHALL manage multiple EventStores in a tree structure with parent-child relationships and metadata per session.
acceptance: SessionTree struct in crates/norn/src/session/tree.rs
SessionId type alias for Uuid
SessionNode struct: id (SessionId), store (EventStore), parent (Option<SessionId>), children (Vec<SessionId>), metadata (SessionMetadata)
SessionMetadata struct: created_at (DateTime<Utc>), model (String), role (Option<String>), status (SessionStatus)
SessionStatus enum: Active, Completed, Failed, Merged
SessionTree::new() creates root session
SessionTree::get(id) -> Option<&SessionNode>
SessionTree::get_store(id) -> Option<&EventStore>
SessionTree::root() -> SessionId
Uses parking_lot::RwLock for concurrent access
Test: create tree, add 3 child sessions, verify parent-child links

requirements.title: Branch operation
requirements.spec: SessionTree::branch SHALL create a child session from a parent, optionally copying events up to a specified point. The child starts with filtered parent context.
acceptance: branch(parent_id, config) -> Result<SessionId> where config includes context_filter
Child EventStore populated from parent events filtered by ContextFilter (same as fork uses)
Parent's children list updated to include the new child
Child's parent set to the parent_id
Fork event appended to parent's store with the child session ID
Test: branch from parent with 10 events, filter to last 5, verify child has 5 events

requirements.title: Merge summary operation
requirements.spec: SessionTree::merge_summary SHALL bring a child session's results back into the parent as a Compaction or summary event, marking the child as Merged.
acceptance: merge_summary(child_id, summary) -> Result<()>
Appends a SessionEvent::Compaction to the parent's store with the summary content
Sets child status to Merged
Child's events remain intact (never deleted — audit trail preserved)
Merge of a non-child returns error
Test: branch, add events to child, merge_summary, verify parent has compaction event

requirements.title: Tree navigation
requirements.spec: SessionTree SHALL support navigating the tree structure: listing children, getting ancestry, and finding sessions by metadata.
acceptance: list_children(id) -> Vec<SessionId>
get_ancestry(id) -> Vec<SessionId> (from root to the given session)
active_sessions() -> Vec<SessionId> (sessions with status Active)
session_count() -> usize
Test: create a 3-level tree (root -> child -> grandchild), verify ancestry returns all 3 in order

requirements.title: Wire fork() to use SessionTree
requirements.spec: The fork() function in agent/fork.rs SHALL register the forked session in the SessionTree instead of creating a disconnected EventStore.
acceptance: fork() gains optional parameter: session_tree (Option<Arc<SessionTree>>)
When session_tree is Some, calls tree.branch() instead of manually copying events
Fork result includes the child session_id from the tree
When session_tree is None, falls back to current behavior (disconnected EventStore)
Test: fork with SessionTree, verify child appears in tree.list_children(parent)

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement session persistence to disk — tree is in-memory
SHALL NOT implement session replay or time-travel debugging
SHALL NOT implement cross-tree merging (merging sessions from different trees)
SHALL NOT implement graph-backed storage (NG3) — this is an in-memory tree structure
SHALL NOT add todo!() or unimplemented!() macros anywhere
verification: cargo test -p norn — all session tests pass
Tree navigation returns correct ancestry — verified by test
merge_summary preserves child events while adding summary to parent — verified by test
fork() creates a session in the tree when SessionTree is provided — verified by test
